mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and weak aggregate demand, triggering recession fears that suppress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, validating corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with > 58% accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly translates to higher earnings expectations and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during production accelerations are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll employment growth demonstrates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, signaling an impending slowdown that depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in > 65% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production combined with persistently low federal funds rates creates an environment of cheap capital and strong output, maximizing corporate margins and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns with > 57% accuracy, or the interaction effect reverses direction during rate hike periods.
